handler.prompt:
When a new session starts, execute the Session-Start sequence:

## SESSION-START SEQUENCE (6-Hook Lifecycle)

### 1. Pre-Run Upgrade
- Check Super-Skill and all sub-skills for available updates
- Upgrade any outdated skills via npx skills
- Search GitHub trending for AI agent best practices
- Integrate discovered patterns

### 2. Anatomy Refresh (OpenWolf)
- Check if anatomy.md exists and is fresh (<24h)
- If stale or missing, scan project to generate/update anatomy.md
- Load anatomy index for efficient file exploration

### 3. Cerebrum Freshness (OpenWolf)
- Check if cerebrum.md was updated in last 7 days
- Load Do-Not-Repeat patterns into context
- Display summary of active learnings

### 4. Token Ledger Init (OpenWolf)
- Initialize new session token ledger
- Load previous session metrics for comparison
- Reset session counters

### 5. Buglog Context (OpenWolf)
- Load recent bug patterns for awareness
- Surface any unresolved bug entries from previous session

### 6. Evolution Sync
- Sync learnings from previous sessions
- Check previous session's experiments.tsv for keep/discard patterns

Report session readiness status before proceeding.

See: .claude/skills/super-skill/skills/pre-run-upgrade/SKILL.md
See: .claude/skills/super-skill/skills/anatomy-scanner/SKILL.md
See: .claude/skills/super-skill/skills/cerebrum/SKILL.md
See: .claude/skills/super-skill/skills/token-tracker/SKILL.md

hooks.command: FILE="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH"; if [ -f "$FILE" ]; then SIZE=$(wc -c < "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); TOKENS=$((SIZE / 4)); echo "[token-tracker] Reading $FILE (~${TOKENS} tokens)" >&2; fi

FILE="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH"; if [ -f "$FILE" ]; then echo "[cerebrum] Pre-write check for $FILE — verify no Do-Not-Repeat patterns" >&2; echo "[buglog] Pre-write check for $FILE — search for related past fixes" >&2; fi

handler.prompt:
## POST-RUN EVOLUTION + OPENWOLF SESSION-END (6-Hook Lifecycle)

Execute after task completion:

### 1. Project Retrospective
- What went well?
- What could be improved?
- Blockers encountered?
- Simplicity assessment: Did we delete more code than we added?

### 2. Signal Extraction
- Defensive: Errors, failures (High priority)
- Opportunity: New patterns (Medium priority)
- Stagnation: No improvement (Low priority)

### 3. Quality Scoring
- Correctness (40%)
- Reliability (25%)
- Maintainability (20%)
- Performance (10%)
- Elegance (5%)

### 4. Evolution Decision
- Score >= 12: Immediate evolution
- 3+ defensive signals: Priority evolution
- Success + new patterns: Capsule creation

### 5. Token Waste Report (OpenWolf)
- Summarize session token usage
- Report waste patterns detected (repeated reads, anatomy-suffice, memory bloat)
- Suggest optimization strategies for next session

### 6. Buglog Summary (OpenWolf)
- Count bug patterns auto-detected during session
- Check if 3+ edits were made without buglog capture
- Warn about missing buglog entries if applicable

### 7. Cerebrum Capture (OpenWolf)
- Capture any new Do-Not-Repeat patterns discovered
- Record user preferences from corrections
- Log key learnings from this session
- Consolidate: merge similar entries, prune stale ones

### 8. Experiment Log Update
- Log all experiments to experiments.tsv
- Summarize keep/discard ratio
- Identify most productive experiment strategies

### 9. Learning Sync
- Update MEMORY.md
- Log to events.jsonl
- Update Context Hub

Generate POST-RUN EVOLUTION REPORT.

See: .claude/skills/super-skill/skills/post-run-evolution/SKILL.md
See: .claude/skills/super-skill/skills/token-tracker/SKILL.md
See: .claude/skills/super-skill/skills/buglog/SKILL.md
See: .claude/skills/super-skill/skills/cerebrum/SKILL.md

hooks.command: FILE="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH"; if echo "$FILE" | grep -qE '\.(css|scss|tsx|jsx)$'; then echo "[design-qc] UI file modified: $FILE — consider visual regression check" >&2; fi
